name: cape-parsers
documentation:
# CAPE-parsers
CAPE core and community parsers

[![PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/CAPE-parsers)](https://pypi.org/project/CAPE-parsers/)

documentation: Helper Functions Go Here
This extracts the Encryption Key and Config File from the Jar and or Dropper

documentation: This extracts the Encryption Key and New conf from a 'Dropper' jar

documentation: Returns only printable chars

functions.name: cape_parsers.deprecated._jRat.messy_split
documentation:
Split on = gives me the right sections but deletes the b64 padding
use modulo math to restore padding.
return new list.
Messy Messy Messy

documentation: Process versions 4.2. >

functions.name: cape_parsers.deprecated._jRat.old_des
documentation: process versions < 3.2.2

Get a list of strings from a section

functions.name: cape_parsers.deprecated.PredatorPain.config_13
documentation:
Identical Strings are not stored multiple times.
We need to check for duplicate passwords which mess up the positionl arguemnts.
Turn the strings in to a python config_dict

functions.name: cape_parsers.deprecated.PredatorPain.config_14
documentation:
Identical Strings are not stored multiple times.
possible pass and date dupes make it harder to test
Turn the strings in to a python config_dict

methods.name: cape_parsers.deprecated.Nymaim.NymaimExtractor.nymaim_decrypt_data_2
documentation: decrypt final config (only raw data, keys passed as parameters)

methods.name: cape_parsers.deprecated.Nymaim.NymaimExtractor.nymaim_extract_blob
documentation: decrypt final config (read keys and length and decrypt raw data)

methods.name: cape_parsers.deprecated.Nymaim.NymaimExtractor.nymaim_parse_blob
documentation: decrypt and interpret config (uses hardcoded hashes)

methods.name: cape_parsers.deprecated.Nymaim.NymaimExtractor.nymaim_brute_blob
documentation:
bruteforce start index of config in decrypted data (decrypted data contains more than config block).
Lame, but should be stable and fast enough.

modules.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.QakBot
documentation: Qakbot decoder for Core/Main DLL

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.QakBot.parse_build
documentation: Extract build version from parent of core dll.

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.QakBot.parse_config
documentation:
Parses the config block into a more human readable format.
Data looks like this initially b'3=1592498872'

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.QakBot.parse_controllers
documentation:
Parses the CNC block into a more human readable format.
    Data looks like this initially 72.29.181.77;0;2078
'

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.QakBot.parse_binary_c2
documentation: Parses the binary CNC block format introduced Nov'20

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.QakBot.parse_binary_c2_2
documentation: Parses the binary CNC block format introduced April'21

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.QakBot.decompress
documentation: Decompress data with blzpack decompression

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.QakBot.decrypt_data
documentation:
Decrypts the data using the last 20 bytes as a rc4 key.
Validates the decryption with the sha1 sum contained within the first 20 bytes of the decrypted data.

modules.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.BumbleBee
documentation: Thanks to @MuziSec - https://github.com/MuziSec/malware_scripts/blob/main/bumblebee/extract_config.py
2024 updates by @enzok

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.BumbleBee.extract_key_data
documentation: Given key match, convert rva to file offset and return key data at that offset.

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.BumbleBee.extract_config_data
documentation:
Given config match, convert rva to file offset and return data at that offset.
The LEA ins are using relative addressing. Referenced data is relative to the address of the NEXT ins.
This is inefficient but I'm bad at Python, okay?

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.core.BumbleBee.extract_config
documentation: Extract key and config and decrypt

modules.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.CobaltStrikeStager
documentation: By Daniel Mayer (Daniel@Stairwell.com), @dan__mayer
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.CobaltStrikeStager.extract_config
documentation: Config extraction function for CapeV2

methods.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.CobaltStrikeStager.StagerConfig.__init__
documentation: f: file path

methods.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.CobaltStrikeStager.StagerConfig._clean
documentation:
s: bytestring to clean
data_type: string determining which cleaning method is appropriate

Converts the bytes of the various stager fields into human-readable settings

methods.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.CobaltStrikeStager.StagerConfig._parse_config
documentation:
Attempts to parse stager config data from a bytes-like object.
The three types of stagers' (HTTP, SMB, DNS) templates
can be found in resources.py

methods.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.CobaltStrikeStager.StagerConfig.get_config
documentation: Returns the settings as a JSON object, or None if none exist

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk._decode_str
documentation:
This function implements the substitution cipher that Nighthawk uses.
Encoded strings are decoded.
Borrowed from <https://www.proofpoint.com/us/blog/threat-insight/nighthawk-and-coming-pentest-tool-likely-gain-threat-actor-notice>
which is no longer available, but here's an archive link:
<https://web.archive.org/web/20221128090619/https://www.proofpoint.com/us/blog/threat-insight/nighthawk-and-coming-pentest-tool-likely-gain-threat-actor-notice>

```
param encoded_string

String encoded with Nighthawk substitution cipher

type encoded_string

<class 'bytes'>

param plaintext_alphabet

Plaintext alphabet used in the substitution cipher

type plaintext_alphabet

<class 'bytes'>

param ciphertext_alphabet

Ciphertext alphabet used in the substitution cipher

type ciphertext_alphabet

<class 'bytes'>

return

Decoded string

rtype

str
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.decode_config_part
documentation:
This function handles each element type of the NightHawk config.
Encoded strings are decoded.
\:param item: config inner item
\:type item: any
\:param plaintext_alphabet: Plaintext alphabet used in the substitution cipher
\:type plaintext_alphabet: \<class 'bytes'>
\:param ciphertext_alphabet: Ciphertext alphabet used in the substitution cipher
\:type ciphertext_alphabet: \<class 'bytes'>
\:return: same object type as the input, but with decoded strings
\:rtype: any

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.decode_config_strings
documentation:
This function implements the substitution cipher that Nighthawk uses.
Encoded strings are decoded.
\:param decrypted_config: Decrypted Nighthawk config
\:type decrypted_config: dict
\:param plaintext_alphabet: Plaintext alphabet used in the substitution cipher
\:type plaintext_alphabet: \<class 'bytes'>
\:param ciphertext_alphabet: Ciphertext alphabet used in the substitution cipher
\:type ciphertext_alphabet: \<class 'bytes'>
\:return: JSON with decoded strings
\:rtype: dict

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk._get_section_data
documentation:
Function to return data belonging to `section_name` section in PE `data`

```
param data

Nighthawk DLL contents

type data

<class 'bytes'>

param section_name

Name of section whose data is to be retrieved

type section_name

str

return

section data

rtype

<class 'bytes'> or None
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk._alphabet_heuristics
documentation:
This function implements heuristics to determine if an identified alphabet
string is actually an alphabet. These heuristics are purely based on my
observations.

```
param alpha

Possible alphabet strings

type alpha

list of <class 'bytes'>

return

set of possible alphabet bytestrings

rtype

set of <class 'bytes'>
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.get_possible_alphabet
documentation:
Nighthawk is known to encode strings using a simple substitution cipher.
Decoding requires knowing the plaintext and ciphertext alphabets used.

```
param data

Nighthawk DLL contents

type data

<class 'bytes'>

return

Permutation of possible plaintext and ciphertext alphabets

rtype

<class 'itertools.permutations'> or None
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.decrypt_config
documentation:
Nighthawk config is gzip compressed and then encrypted with AES-128 CBC mode.

```
param encrypted_config

Encrypted config data

type encrypted_config

<class 'bytes'>

param decryption_key

Config decryption key

type decryption_key

<class 'bytes'>

return

decrypted config

rtype

dict or None
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.get_encoded_config
documentation:
The contents of Nighthawk DLL .profile section contain 4 components:

: 1. Keying method

  2\. Config decryption key (optional)
  2\. Size of configuration
  3\. Encrypted configuration

At this point, it is confirmed that the keying method == 0 and config
decryption key is available in the .profile section.

```
param data

Nighthawk DLL .profile section contents

type data

<class 'bytes'>

return

Encrypted config data

rtype

<class 'bytes'> or None
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.get_decryption_key
documentation:
The contents of Nighthawk DLL .profile section contain 4 components:

: 1. Keying method

  2\. Config decryption key (optional)
  2\. Size of configuration
  3\. Encrypted configuration

```
param data

Nighthawk DLL .profile section contents

type data

<class 'bytes'>

return

Config decryption key

rtype

<class 'bytes'> or None
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.get_profile_section_contents
documentation:
Nighthawk DLLs are known to contain a .profile section which contains
configuration information.

```
param data

Nighthawk DLL contents

type data

<class 'bytes'>

return

.profile section contents

rtype

<class 'bytes'> or None
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.get_last_text_section
documentation:
Newer Nighthawk DLLs are known to contain a .text at the end which contains
configuration information.

```
param data

Nighthawk DLL contents

type data

<class 'bytes'>

return

last .text section contents

rtype

<class 'bytes'> or None
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.get_config_section_content
documentation:
Get the config section data either from .profile, or from the last .text section
which is available

```
param data

Nighthawk DLL contents

type data

<class 'bytes'>

return

the config data contents

rtype

<class 'bytes'> or None
```

functions.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Nighthawk.extract_config
documentation:
Configuration extractor for Nighthawk DLL

```
param data

Nighthawk DLL contents

type data

<class 'bytes'>

return

Decrypted and decoded config

rtype

dict or None
```

methods.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Njrat.Parser.get_user_string_from_index

methods.name: cape_parsers.CAPE.community.Njrat.Parser.get_field_name_from_index
documentation: in little-endian token is: 12 00 00 04 (0x40000012), where 0x04 is field table index, and 0x12 is the field index

```
bytes

rtype

bytes
```
